stateKeys.name: status
stateKeys.type: enum: 'not_paid' | 'awaiting' | 'authorized' | 'partially_authorized' | 'canceled' — the exact five members declared at v2.4.0 in packages/core/types/src/payment/common.ts

stateKeys.name: hasSessions
stateKeys.type: boolean — ABSTRACTION of payment_sessions.length; the derivation only tests === 0

stateKeys.name: authorized
stateKeys.type: enum: 'none' | 'partial' | 'full' — ABSTRACTION of authorizedAmount; the derivation only tests > 0 and >= amount

stateKeys.name: captured
stateKeys.type: enum: 'none' | 'partial' | 'full' — ABSTRACTION of captured_amount; read by NO status decision at v2.4.0

stateKeys.name: refunded
stateKeys.type: enum: 'none' | 'partial' | 'full' — ABSTRACTION of refunded_amount; read by NO status decision at v2.4.0

specialRules.name: no-session-to-authorize
specialRules.note: authorizePaymentSession operates on a session id; with no session there is nothing to authorize. The precondition is on the SESSION entity, not on PaymentCollection.status.

specialRules.name: nothing-authorized-to-capture
specialRules.note: capturePayment operates on a Payment created from an authorized session. capturePayment_ throws when payment.canceled_at is set. Again a sibling-entity guard, not a collection-status guard.

specialRules.name: nothing-captured-to-refund
specialRules.note: refundPayment requires a capture to refund against.

noOpRule: An action that does not apply is reject(reason) — an observable no-op, never a throw. NOTE that at v2.4.0 CANCEL_ORDER has NO rejection path: cancelOrderWorkflow stamps every linked collection unconditionally, and cancelling twice is refused by nothing in the payment module.
abstractionBound: Money is abstracted to none/partial/full and session count to a boolean. Medusa uses BigNumber amounts and an unbounded session list; the status derivation distinguishes only `authorizedAmount > 0` and `authorizedAmount >= amount`, and only `sessions.length === 0`, so these abstractions are lossless FOR THE STATUS LOGIC and lossy for everything else. They are what makes exploration finite.
projectionBound: These five keys are the ENTIRE observable state. Provider ids, payment-session data blobs, currency, refund reasons, individual capture/refund records, `completed_at`, metadata, and the whole Order and Cart that own the collection are OUTSIDE the projection: not checked, not migrated, not audited. Every guarantee made here is relative to this list.
